picks.rationale: MPC has surged +61% YTD and +58% 1-year with strong 1M/+6.22% momentum after a Morgan Stanley PT raise. Its P/E of 17.36 is reasonable for the refining sector, with news sentiment very positive at 0.485 and a buy consensus.

VLO leads the refining peer group with +88% 1-year and +58% YTD returns, positive 1M/+6.47% momentum, and the highest news sentiment (0.554) in the universe after a Morgan Stanley PT raise. P/E of 18.91 is attractive given strong earnings momentum.

SLB has excellent momentum across all timeframes (1D/+1.24%, 1W/+2.39%, 1M/+1.98%) with +41% YTD and a best-in-class analyst score of 1.62 (strong buy consensus). The oilfield services leader benefits directly from sustained upstream capex amid rising oil prices.

SHEL has +15.65% YTD with a P/E of 13.34, a solid 3.65% dividend yield, and positive news sentiment of 0.265. The CEO forecasts rising oil prices over 5-10 years, while the current buyback pause for the ARC acquisition supports a long-term growth narrative.

ENPH has exploded +61.75% YTD with stunning 1M/+29.98% momentum, driven by surging solar demand as ARRAY Technologies hits 100GW. Despite a high P/E of 54 and 24.43% short interest, momentum and a buyable pullback make it a compelling high-risk/high-reward pick.

EOG shows +29.6% YTD and +1.27% 1M momentum with an attractive P/E of 13.44 and a 2.99% dividend yield. The pure-play E&P benefits from ongoing Middle East turmoil — the latest Israel-Iran strikes sent Brent +3% and create a direct earnings catalyst for upstream producers.

CVX has +22.38% YTD with positive 1M/+1.58% momentum and a buy consensus (an_score 1.84). The oil major offers direct Brent leverage amid escalating Israel-Iran conflict, though a relatively high P/E of 32.56 tempers confidence. Its 3.8% dividend adds defensiveness.

FSLR trades at a reasonable P/E of 17.27 and has rebounded +13.94% over 1M after a weak YTD. Its buy consensus and 0.246 news sentiment reflect strong solar demand tailwinds, though 10.39% short interest warrants caution.

SSE.L has +7.58% YTD and strong 1M/+5.68% momentum with a 2.86% dividend yield. The UK-Japan £18bn investment agreement and SSE's positioning in UK power grids and renewables provide steady, rate-sensitive upside with a buy consensus.

OXY has strong YTD growth (+34.66%) with very high news sentiment (0.478) and a positive 1D/+1.98% move. The company is partnering with Exxon in Trinidad and has Gulf of Mexico discoveries. However, a high P/E of 76.41 limits upside relative to peers.
